name: Berkshire (United Kingdom)

colors.symbolism: the oak's foliage and the grass

description: A gold field bearing a naturally-coloured brown stag standing beneath a green-leaved oak tree on a grassy mount — the traditional Stag and Oak of the Royal County of Berkshire.
history: Registered with the Flag Institute in 2017 after a public competition. The stag (a twelve-point 'royal' hart) beneath an oak refers to the county's 'Royal County' title and to a symbol associated with Berkshire since at least Michael Drayton's 1627 poem.

features.symbolism: Windsor Forest and the Royal Oak
